{{nihongo|'''Mononobe Shrine'''|物部神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

石神神社
山之神名古屋市教育委員会が作成した、境内にある立て板の説明による。境内にある由緒記の説明による。 | 例祭 = 10月7日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''物部神社'''（もののべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「物部神社」に比定されているが、後述するように異説もある。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の時代に創建されたという伝をもつ古社で、かつては広い境内を持っていたという。大石を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}とし、{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''石神堂'''あるいは'''山之神'''などと呼ばれていた。{{jalink|17世紀|Q7016}}中頃に成立した『寛文村々覚書』には「古井村...山之神」と書かれており、17世紀の時点では{{jalink|山神}}として祀られていたらしい。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には、{{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}}が荒廃していた社殿を修復したと伝わる。 神体となっている「要石」と呼ばれる大石は社殿下にあるといい、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地を平定した際に{{jalink|国|Q6256}}の鎮めとしたという伝説がある。 江戸期に{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}が当社を『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある尾張国愛知郡「物部神社」に比定し、以降は定説となった。しかし天野が当社を物部神社に比定した根拠は、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の祖神である{{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}を祭神とする点のみで、{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}などは残っていない。江戸後期の{{jalink|郷土史家}}である{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}は、当社を「物部神社」に比定することを批判し、本来の「物部神社」は{{jalink|御器所八幡宮|Q3109957}}であるとしている。{{jalink|考古学|Q23498}}的にみても当地周辺に古代の遺跡は少ない一方、{{jalink|御器所|Q50984787}}周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}や{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}が散在するため、『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』にある愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|物部郷}}を御器所周辺に比定する説が有力視されている{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）に当社が正式に式内社の「物部神社」に比定され、村社に列せられたが、現在は無格社となっている。{{jalink|1953年|Q18585}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}28年）には本殿拝殿が造営された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}（うましまじのみこと） === ギャラリー === Nagoya city 103.jpg|2021年5月 Nagoya city 104.jpg|2021年5月 === 所在地・交通 === * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}筒井三丁目31番21号 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|中央線 (名古屋地区)|Q11363767|中央本線}} {{jalink|千種駅|Q863068}}より北に300m。 * {{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄|Q1144874}}{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄東山線|Q1132799|東山線}} 千種駅より北に300m。 * 名古屋市営地下鉄{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄桜通線|Q1132780|桜通線}} {{jalink|車道駅|Q858916}}よりすぐ。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === === 外部リンク === possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|論]] vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''物部神社'''（もののべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「物部神社」に比定されているが、後述するように異説もある。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の時代に創建されたという伝をもつ古社で、かつては広い境内を持っていたという。大石を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}とし、{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''石神堂'''あるいは'''山之神'''などと呼ばれていた。{{jalink|17世紀|Q7016}}中頃に成立した『寛文村々覚書』には「古井村...山之神」と書かれており、17世紀の時点では{{jalink|山神}}として祀られていたらしい。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には、{{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}}が荒廃していた社殿を修復したと伝わる。 神体となっている「要石」と呼ばれる大石は社殿下にあるといい、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地を平定した際に{{jalink|国|Q6256}}の鎮めとしたという伝説がある。 江戸期に{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}が当社を『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある尾張国愛知郡「物部神社」に比定し、以降は定説となった。しかし天野が当社を物部神社に比定した根拠は、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の祖神である{{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}を祭神とする点のみで、{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}などは残っていない。江戸後期の{{jalink|郷土史家}}である{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}は、当社を「物部神社」に比定することを批判し、本来の「物部神社」は{{jalink|御器所八幡宮|Q3109957}}であるとしている。{{jalink|考古学|Q23498}}的にみても当地周辺に古代の遺跡は少ない一方、{{jalink|御器所|Q50984787}}周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}や{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}が散在するため、『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』にある愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|物部郷}}を御器所周辺に比定する説が有力視されている{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）に当社が正式に式内社の「物部神社」に比定され、村社に列せられたが、現在は無格社となっている。{{jalink|1953年|Q18585}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}28年）には本殿拝殿が造営された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}（うましまじのみこと） === ギャラリー === Nagoya city 103.jpg|2021年5月 Nagoya city 104.jpg|2021年5月 === 所在地・交通 === * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}筒井三丁目31番21号 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|中央線 (名古屋地区)|Q11363767|中央本線}} {{jalink|千種駅|Q863068}}より北に300m。 * {{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄|Q1144874}}{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄東山線|Q1132799|東山線}} 千種駅より北に300m。 * 名古屋市営地下鉄{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄桜通線|Q1132780|桜通線}} {{jalink|車道駅|Q858916}}よりすぐ。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === === 外部リンク === 鳥居 | 画像の説明 = 物部神社の鳥居 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}三丁目31番21号 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}} | 社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|式内社}}論社 | 創建 = （伝）{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の治世 | 本殿 = | 別名 = 石神堂
石神神社
山之神名古屋市教育委員会が作成した、境内にある立て板の説明による。境内にある由緒記の説明による。 | 例祭 = 10月7日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''物部神社'''（もののべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「物部神社」に比定されているが、後述するように異説もある。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の時代に創建されたという伝をもつ古社で、かつては広い境内を持っていたという。大石を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}とし、{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''石神堂'''あるいは'''山之神'''などと呼ばれていた。{{jalink|17世紀|Q7016}}中頃に成立した『寛文村々覚書』には「古井村...山之神」と書かれており、17世紀の時点では{{jalink|山神}}として祀られていたらしい。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には、{{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}}が荒廃していた社殿を修復したと伝わる。 神体となっている「要石」と呼ばれる大石は社殿下にあるといい、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地を平定した際に{{jalink|国|Q6256}}の鎮めとしたという伝説がある。 江戸期に{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}が当社を『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある尾張国愛知郡「物部神社」に比定し、以降は定説となった。しかし天野が当社を物部神社に比定した根拠は、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の祖神である{{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}を祭神とする点のみで、{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}などは残っていない。江戸後期の{{jalink|郷土史家}}である{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}は、当社を「物部神社」に比定することを批判し、本来の「物部神社」は{{jalink|御器所八幡宮|Q3109957}}であるとしている。{{jalink|考古学|Q23498}}的にみても当地周辺に古代の遺跡は少ない一方、{{jalink|御器所|Q50984787}}周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}や{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}が散在するため、『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』にある愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|物部郷}}を御器所周辺に比定する説が有力視されている{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）に当社が正式に式内社の「物部神社」に比定され、村社に列せられたが、現在は無格社となっている。{{jalink|1953年|Q18585}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}28年）には本殿拝殿が造営された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}（うましまじのみこと） === ギャラリー === Nagoya city 103.jpg|2021年5月 Nagoya city 104.jpg|2021年5月 === 所在地・交通 === * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}筒井三丁目31番21号 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|中央線 (名古屋地区)|Q11363767|中央本線}} {{jalink|千種駅|Q863068}}より北に300m。 * {{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄|Q1144874}}{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄東山線|Q1132799|東山線}} 千種駅より北に300m。 * 名古屋市営地下鉄{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄桜通線|Q1132780|桜通線}} {{jalink|車道駅|Q858916}}よりすぐ。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もののへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Mononobe-jinja (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)}} [[JA:物部神社 (名古屋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q18235752}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 物部神社 | 画像 = [[File:Mononobe-jinja ac (1).jpg|250px]]
鳥居 | 画像の説明 = 物部神社の鳥居 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}三丁目31番21号 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}} | 社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|式内社}}論社 | 創建 = （伝）{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の治世 | 本殿 = | 別名 = 石神堂
石神神社
山之神名古屋市教育委員会が作成した、境内にある立て板の説明による。境内にある由緒記の説明による。 | 例祭 = 10月7日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''物部神社'''（もののべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「物部神社」に比定されているが、後述するように異説もある。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の時代に創建されたという伝をもつ古社で、かつては広い境内を持っていたという。大石を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}とし、{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''石神堂'''あるいは'''山之神'''などと呼ばれていた。{{jalink|17世紀|Q7016}}中頃に成立した『寛文村々覚書』には「古井村...山之神」と書かれており、17世紀の時点では{{jalink|山神}}として祀られていたらしい。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には、{{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}}が荒廃していた社殿を修復したと伝わる。 神体となっている「要石」と呼ばれる大石は社殿下にあるといい、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地を平定した際に{{jalink|国|Q6256}}の鎮めとしたという伝説がある。 江戸期に{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}が当社を『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある尾張国愛知郡「物部神社」に比定し、以降は定説となった。しかし天野が当社を物部神社に比定した根拠は、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の祖神である{{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}を祭神とする点のみで、{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}などは残っていない。江戸後期の{{jalink|郷土史家}}である{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}は、当社を「物部神社」に比定することを批判し、本来の「物部神社」は{{jalink|御器所八幡宮|Q3109957}}であるとしている。{{jalink|考古学|Q23498}}的にみても当地周辺に古代の遺跡は少ない一方、{{jalink|御器所|Q50984787}}周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}や{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}が散在するため、『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』にある愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|物部郷}}を御器所周辺に比定する説が有力視されている{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）に当社が正式に式内社の「物部神社」に比定され、村社に列せられたが、現在は無格社となっている。{{jalink|1953年|Q18585}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}28年）には本殿拝殿が造営された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}（うましまじのみこと） === ギャラリー === Nagoya city 103.jpg|2021年5月 Nagoya city 104.jpg|2021年5月 === 所在地・交通 === * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}筒井三丁目31番21号 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|中央線 (名古屋地区)|Q11363767|中央本線}} {{jalink|千種駅|Q863068}}より北に300m。 * {{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄|Q1144874}}{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄東山線|Q1132799|東山線}} 千種駅より北に300m。 * 名古屋市営地下鉄{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄桜通線|Q1132780|桜通線}} {{jalink|車道駅|Q858916}}よりすぐ。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もののへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:物部氏|社]] [[Category:筒井 (名古屋市)]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMononobe Shrine (Q18235752)}}

ac (1).jpg|250px]]
鳥居 | 画像の説明 = 物部神社の鳥居 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}三丁目31番21号 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}} | 社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|式内社}}論社 | 創建 = （伝）{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の治世 | 本殿 = | 別名 = 石神堂
石神神社
山之神名古屋市教育委員会が作成した、境内にある立て板の説明による。境内にある由緒記の説明による。 | 例祭 = 10月7日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''物部神社'''（もののべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「物部神社」に比定されているが、後述するように異説もある。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の時代に創建されたという伝をもつ古社で、かつては広い境内を持っていたという。大石を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}とし、{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''石神堂'''あるいは'''山之神'''などと呼ばれていた。{{jalink|17世紀|Q7016}}中頃に成立した『寛文村々覚書』には「古井村...山之神」と書かれており、17世紀の時点では{{jalink|山神}}として祀られていたらしい。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には、{{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}}が荒廃していた社殿を修復したと伝わる。 神体となっている「要石」と呼ばれる大石は社殿下にあるといい、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地を平定した際に{{jalink|国|Q6256}}の鎮めとしたという伝説がある。 江戸期に{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}が当社を『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある尾張国愛知郡「物部神社」に比定し、以降は定説となった。しかし天野が当社を物部神社に比定した根拠は、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の祖神である{{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}を祭神とする点のみで、{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}などは残っていない。江戸後期の{{jalink|郷土史家}}である{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}は、当社を「物部神社」に比定することを批判し、本来の「物部神社」は{{jalink|御器所八幡宮|Q3109957}}であるとしている。{{jalink|考古学|Q23498}}的にみても当地周辺に古代の遺跡は少ない一方、{{jalink|御器所|Q50984787}}周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}や{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}が散在するため、『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』にある愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|物部郷}}を御器所周辺に比定する説が有力視されている{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）に当社が正式に式内社の「物部神社」に比定され、村社に列せられたが、現在は無格社となっている。{{jalink|1953年|Q18585}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}28年）には本殿拝殿が造営された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}（うましまじのみこと） === ギャラリー === Nagoya city 103.jpg|2021年5月 Nagoya city 104.jpg|2021年5月 === 所在地・交通 === * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}筒井三丁目31番21号 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|中央線 (名古屋地区)|Q11363767|中央本線}} {{jalink|千種駅|Q863068}}より北に300m。 * {{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄|Q1144874}}{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄東山線|Q1132799|東山線}} 千種駅より北に300m。 * 名古屋市営地下鉄{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄桜通線|Q1132780|桜通線}} {{jalink|車道駅|Q858916}}よりすぐ。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === === 外部リンク === ac (1).jpg|250px]]
鳥居 | 画像の説明 = 物部神社の鳥居 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}三丁目31番21号 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}} | 社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
{{jalink|式内社}}論社 | 創建 = （伝）{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の治世 | 本殿 = | 別名 = 石神堂
石神神社
山之神名古屋市教育委員会が作成した、境内にある立て板の説明による。境内にある由緒記の説明による。 | 例祭 = 10月7日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=物部神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''物部神社'''（もののべじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}{{jalink|筒井 (名古屋市)|Q62588744|筒井}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「物部神社」に比定されているが、後述するように異説もある。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}の時代に創建されたという伝をもつ古社で、かつては広い境内を持っていたという。大石を{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}とし、{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''石神堂'''あるいは'''山之神'''などと呼ばれていた。{{jalink|17世紀|Q7016}}中頃に成立した『寛文村々覚書』には「古井村...山之神」と書かれており、17世紀の時点では{{jalink|山神}}として祀られていたらしい。{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}年間には、{{jalink|徳川綱誠|Q982743}}が荒廃していた社殿を修復したと伝わる。 神体となっている「要石」と呼ばれる大石は社殿下にあるといい、{{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}がこの地を平定した際に{{jalink|国|Q6256}}の鎮めとしたという伝説がある。 江戸期に{{jalink|天野信景|Q6535030}}が当社を『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある尾張国愛知郡「物部神社」に比定し、以降は定説となった。しかし天野が当社を物部神社に比定した根拠は、{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の祖神である{{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}を祭神とする点のみで、{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}などは残っていない。江戸後期の{{jalink|郷土史家}}である{{jalink|津田正生|Q22123474}}は、当社を「物部神社」に比定することを批判し、本来の「物部神社」は{{jalink|御器所八幡宮|Q3109957}}であるとしている。{{jalink|考古学|Q23498}}的にみても当地周辺に古代の遺跡は少ない一方、{{jalink|御器所|Q50984787}}周辺には{{jalink|古墳|Q1141225}}や{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}が散在するため、『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』にある愛知郡{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)#郷|Q552687|物部郷}}を御器所周辺に比定する説が有力視されている{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}。 {{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年）に当社が正式に式内社の「物部神社」に比定され、村社に列せられたが、現在は無格社となっている。{{jalink|1953年|Q18585}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}28年）には本殿拝殿が造営された。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|ウマシマジ|Q10946169|宇麻志麻遲命}}（うましまじのみこと） === ギャラリー === Nagoya city 103.jpg|2021年5月 Nagoya city 104.jpg|2021年5月 === 所在地・交通 === * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|東区 (名古屋市)|Q578824|東区}}筒井三丁目31番21号 * {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|中央線 (名古屋地区)|Q11363767|中央本線}} {{jalink|千種駅|Q863068}}より北に300m。 * {{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄|Q1144874}}{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄東山線|Q1132799|東山線}} 千種駅より北に300m。 * 名古屋市営地下鉄{{jalink|名古屋市営地下鉄桜通線|Q1132780|桜通線}} {{jalink|車道駅|Q858916}}よりすぐ。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もののへしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] AI-assisted translation: Japanese wiki content translated by local AI agents (deepseek-r1:8b via Ollama, reviewed/assembled by Claude Code). Quality varies — some pages may contain subtle translation errors.

1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page
1 revision imported: Bot: batch import AI-translated shrine pages from jawiki

Text replacement - "|lt=Q" to "|ltq=Q"
Bot: fix ill template destination links

category|Category:Mononobe-jinja (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)}} [[JA:物部神社 (名古屋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q18235752}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Mononobe Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Mononobe-jinja ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|WD=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|WD=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|WD=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|WD=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * (1).jpg|250px]]
Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * (1).jpg|250px]]
Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|WD=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|WD=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|WD=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|WD=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === {{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * (1).jpg|250px]]
Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|WD=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|WD=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|WD=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|WD=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|WD=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|WD=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|WD=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|WD=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === === External links === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市東区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市東区の建築物]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:物部氏|社]] [[Category:筒井 (名古屋市)]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|WD=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|WD=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|WD=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|WD=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === === External links === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市東区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市東区の建築物]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:物部氏|社]] [[Category:筒井 (名古屋市)]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]
