Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|WD=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|WD=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|WD=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|WD=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|WD=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|WD=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|WD=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|WD=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|WD=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|WD=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|WD=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|WD=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === === External links === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:History of Higashi ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashi ward]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:物部氏|社]] [[Category:筒井 (名古屋市)]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|WD=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|WD=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|WD=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|WD=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|WD=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|WD=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|WD=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|WD=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === === External links === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:History of Higashi ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashi ward]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:物部氏|社]] [[Category:筒井 (名古屋市)]]

Torii | 画像の説明 = Torii of Mononobe Shrine | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|qid=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|qid=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}3-31-21 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|qid=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}
{{ill|lt=candidate Shikinaisha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} | 創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} | 本殿 = | 別名 = Ishigami-dō
Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|qid=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|qid=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|qid=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|qid=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|qid=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|qid=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|qid=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|qid=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|qid=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|qid=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|qid=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|qid=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|qid=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|qid=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|qid=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|qid=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|qid=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === === External links === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:History of Higashi ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashi ward]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:物部氏|社]] [[Category:筒井 (名古屋市)]]

Ishigami Shrine
Yama-no-KamiAccording to signboard text in the precinct prepared by Nagoya City Board of Education.According to the shrine-origin text in the precinct. | 例祭 = October 7 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Mononobe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mononobe Shrine''' (もののべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tsutsui (Nagoya)|Q62588744|qid=Q62588744|lt=Tsutsui|1=Tsutsui}}, {{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|qid=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It has been identified with "Mononobe Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' but as noted below, there are differing views. === History === It is an old shrine with a tradition of founding in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and is said to have once had broad precinct grounds. With a large stone as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, it was called '''Ishigami-dō''' or '''Yama-no-Kami''' until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|qid=Q184963}}. The '{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}' compiled around the mid-{{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|qid=Q7016|1=17th century}} records "Furui Village ... Yama-no-Kami," suggesting it was worshiped as a mountain deity at that time. In the {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunashige|Q982743|qid=Q982743|1=Tokugawa Tsunanari}} is said to have repaired the dilapidated shrine buildings. The large stone called "Kaname-ishi," treated as sacred object, is said to be under the shrine building, with a legend that {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} used it to pacify the {{ill|lt=land|Q6256|qid=Q6256|1=Country}} when subduing this area. In the Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine as the Owari Aichi District "Mononobe Shrine" listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and this became the prevailing view. However, his basis was only that its deity was {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|qid=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}}, ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, and no materials such as {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} survive. Late-Edo local historian {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} criticized identifying this shrine as Mononobe Shrine and argued the original Mononobe Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gokiso Hachimangū|Q3109957|qid=Q3109957|1=Gokiso Hachimangū}}. Archaeologically, few ancient remains are found around this site, whereas around {{ill|lt=Gokiso|Q50984787|qid=Q50984787|1=Gokiso}} kofun and abandoned temple sites are scattered; therefore, the theory identifying Mononobe-gō in Aichi District of the '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|qid=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' with the Gokiso area is considered stronger{{Sfn|名古屋郷土文化会|1986|p=5}}. In {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1), this shrine was formally identified with the Shikinaisha "Mononobe Shrine" and ranked village shrine, but today it is unranked. In {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|qid=Q18585|1=1953}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 28), the honden and haiden were built. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Umashimaji|Q10946169|qid=Q10946169|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=Umashimaji}} (うましまじのみこと) === Gallery === Nagoya city 103.jpg|May 2021 Nagoya city 104.jpg|May 2021 === Location / Access === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-ku (Nagoya)|Q578824|qid=Q578824|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku, Nagoya}} Tsutsui 3-31-21 * 300 m north from {{ill|lt=Chikusa Station|Q863068|qid=Q863068|1=Chikusa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Chūō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11363767|qid=Q11363767|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō line}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} * 300 m north from Chikusa Station on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Higashiyama Line|Q1132799|qid=Q1132799|lt=Higashiyama Line|1=Higashiyama Line}} * Immediately near {{ill|lt=Kurumamichi Station|Q858916|qid=Q858916|1=Kurumamichi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Sakuradōri Line|Q1132780|qid=Q1132780|lt=Sakuradōri Line|1=Sakura-dōri Line}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === References list === === External links === {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mononobe Shrine (Higashi-ku, Nagoya)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:History of Higashi ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashi ward]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:物部氏|社]] [[Category:筒井 (名古屋市)]]
